benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to RS&H employees.

benefitChapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
benefitChapters.description: Overview of all available benefit plans for RS&H employees.

children.text: Review and compare all benefit plans available to RS&H employees.
Medical Plans

Your medical benefits are provided by BlueCross BlueShield and include coverage for both in-network and out-of-network providers. All plans participate in the same network, and you may choose your provider. Choosing a provider that is in network provide you with significant savings.
3 medical plans available: PPO Plan, Prime HDHP, Alternate HDHP
Dental Plans

Dental plans cover diagnostic and preventive care, plus basic and major services. Although you can choose any dental provider, you will generally pay less when you visit an in-network dentist. If you choose an out-of-network provider, you may be billed the difference between what MetLife pays, and what your out-of-network provider charges for the services. To locate an in-network provider, please visit www.metlife.com/dental.

With a MetLife Plus dentist, you’ll enjoy: Quality Assurance: Dentists are monitored for proper licensing, cleanliness and safety. No balance billing: You won’t be charged more than the contracted rate. No pre-payment: You’ll pay only your portion of the bill - insurance pays your dentist directly. Lower prices: Through reduced fees.
2 dental plans available: Core Plan and Enhanced Plan
Vision Plans

Our vision care benefits include coverage for eye exams, lenses and frames, contact lenses, and discounts for laser surgery. The vision plan is built around the network providers who offer you higher benefits at a lower cost. Consider using an in-network provider for the most bang for your buck when you need services! For out-of-network providers, you will be reimbursed for services according to the grid below.
2 vision plans available: Core VSP and Enhanced VSP

benefitChapters.title: Eligibility & Qualifying Life Events
benefitChapters.description: Information about who is eligible for benefits, eligible dependents, enrollment, and qualifying life events.

children.text: Benefits are available to all full-time part-time associates (minimum 20 hours per week) and their dependents. If you enroll during Open Enrollment, your benefits will become effective on January 1, 2026. If you are newly hired, your benefits will become effective on the first day of the month following your date of hire.
Eligibility

Benefits are available to all full-time part-time associates (minimum 20 hours per week) and their dependents. If you enroll during Open Enrollment, your benefits will become effective on January 1, 2026. If you are newly hired, your benefits will become effective on the first day of the month following your date of hire.
A full-time or part-time associate working a minimum of 20 hours per week.
Eligible Dependents
Spouse or Domestic Partner

Your children from birth to age 26 (includes natural, legally adopted, stepchildren and/or unmarried dependent children of any age who are mentally and physically disabled and dependent on you for support).
Enrollment
Open Enrollment: annually during the open enrollment period.
New Hire: must enroll within 10 days of hire.
Qualified Life Event: changes must be made within 30 days of life event.
Common Qualifying Events
Changes in your life may permit you to update your coverage at points throughout the year, outside of the open enrollment period.
Changes to coverage must be made within 30 days of when the life event occurs. Proof of the life event is required.
Birth, legal adoption, or placement for adoption.
Change in marital status.
Dependent child reaches age 26.
Spouse gains or loses employment or eligibility with current employer.
Death of a covered dependent.
Spouse or dependent becomes eligible or ineligible for Medicare/Medicaid or SCHIP.
Change in residence that changes eligibility for coverage.
Court-ordered change.

benefitChapters.title: PPO Plan Medical Plan
benefitChapters.description: The PPO Plan medical benefits are provided by BlueCross BlueShield including in-network and out-of-network coverage.

children.text: The PPO Plan medical benefits are provided by BlueCross BlueShield including in-network and out-of-network coverage.
Copays
Copays are fixed dollar amounts you pay for certain in-network services on a PPO-type plan.
Deductible
Deductible is the amount you must pay before your plan begins paying for services covered by coinsurance.
Family deductible amounts are $6,000 (in-network) and $13,500 (out-of-network).
Out-of-Pocket Maximum
Out-of-pocket maximum is the most you must pay for covered services in a plan year.
Family out-of-pocket maximum amounts are $12,000 (in-network) and $18,000 (out-of-network).
In-network vs Out-of-network
**In-network**
A group of doctors, clinics, hospitals, and other healthcare providers have an agreement with your medical plan provider.
You pay a negotiated rate for services when you use in-network providers.
**Out-of-network**
Care received from a doctor, hospital, or other provider not part of the plan agreement.
You’ll pay more when you use out-of-network providers since they don’t have a negotiated rate with your plan provider.
You may also be billed the difference between what the out-of-network provider charges for services and what the plan provider pays.
To check if a provider is in-network, visit the BlueCross BlueShield website or use their provider search tool.
Help with Selecting Your Medical Coverage
ALEX is the official RS&H benefits counselor that provides personalized, confidential benefits guidance and clear explanations to help you select the best coverage options.

Find an in-network pharmacy or use the drug cost estimator tool by visiting www.optumrx.com. Discount sites like GoodRx and WellRx provide instant savings. Ask your provider or pharmacist if a generic/mail order is available. Generic contraceptives and diaphragms are covered in full.
Teladoc Virtual Visit is available at no cost for PPO Plan.
**Toolkit App**: MyHealthToolkit

benefitChapters.description: The Prime HDHP medical plan is a High Deductible Health Plan eligible for HSA contributions.

children.text: The Prime HDHP medical plan is a High Deductible Health Plan eligible for HSA contributions.

Copays are 20% after deductible for convenience clinic visit and other specified services.
Deductible
The deductible is $1,800 single coverage and $3,600 family coverage for in-network.
Out-of-network deductible is $3,000 individual and $6,000 family.
Out-of-Pocket Maximum
Out-of-pocket maximum is $4,500 single coverage and $9,000 family coverage for in-network.
Out-of-network out-of-pocket maximum is $6,000 individual and $12,000 family.
In-network vs Out-of-network
**In-network**
A group of doctors, clinics, hospitals, and other healthcare providers have an agreement with your medical plan provider.
You pay a negotiated rate for services when you use in-network providers.
**Out-of-network**

You’ll pay more when you use out-of-network providers since they don’t have a negotiated rate with your plan provider.
You may also be billed the difference between what the out-of-network provider charges for services and what the plan provider pays.
To check if a provider is in-network, visit the BlueCross BlueShield website or use their provider search tool.
Help with Selecting Your Medical Coverage
ALEX is the official RS&H benefits counselor that provides personalized, confidential benefits guidance and clear explanations to help you select the best coverage options.

Find an in-network pharmacy or use the drug cost estimator tool by visiting www.optumrx.com. Discount sites like GoodRx and WellRx provide instant savings. Ask your provider or pharmacist if a generic/mail order is available. Generic contraceptives and diaphragms are covered in full.
Teladoc Virtual Visit is available at no cost for PPO Plan.

benefitChapters.title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
benefitChapters.description: RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. You can access Health Advocate services 24/7 with a licensed, professio

children.text: RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. You can access Health Advocate services 24/7 with a licensed, professional counselor available for immediate assistance.
Services are available 24/7 via telephone, face-to-face, and web-based assistance.
Overview

RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) tailored to your needs. You can access Health Advocate services 24/7 with a licensed, professional counselor available for immediate assistance.
Free Visits
Six free visits (virtual or in person) with licensed professionals per incident.
Services include:
adoption

anxiety
buying a car or home
cancer

benefitChapters.title: Health Savings Account (HSA) & Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
benefitChapters.description: HSA and FSA account options for tax-advantaged savings.

children.text: Understand your tax-advantaged savings account options.
What is a Health Savings Account?
A Health Savings Account (HSA) is designed to be paired with a qualified High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP), like the Prime or Alternate HDHP, to save for current and future healthcare needs.
Triple Tax Advantage: Contributions, interest earned, and withdrawals for qualified expenses are tax-free.
Benefits of HSA
You can use the account to pay for qualified healthcare expenses.
Unspent dollars roll over each year and are yours to keep, even if you retire or leave the company.
You can invest your HSA funds so your available healthcare dollars can grow over time.
Who is eligible for an HSA?
You are eligible if you are enrolled in the HDHP.
You are not covered by a spouse’s plan.
No one else can claim you as a dependent.
You are not enrolled in Medicare, TRICARE, or TRICARE for Life.
You have not received VA benefits in the past 3 months.
How to manage your HSA account
Access and manage your HSA through your Fidelity NetBenefit account at NetBenefits.com.
During enrollment, you will establish your contribution amount in the benefits portal.
You can make changes to your contribution amount anytime throughout the year through the benefits portal.
What is a Flexible Spending Account?

A flexible spending account (FSA) is a tax-advantaged account that can reimburse you for qualified healthcare or dependent care expenses. You can fund qualified expenses with pre-tax dollars deducted from your paycheck. For HDHP Prime & HDHP Alternate medical plan enrollees, your Limited Purpose Health Care FSA may only be used for eligible dental and vision expenses.
Understanding different types of FSAs
Your Limited Purpose FSA can only be used for eligible dental and vision expenses.
Dependent Care FSA can be used for dependent care expenses, such as babysitters and daycare.
Claims must be incurred between January 1 and December 31, and submitted by March 15.
Up to $660 of unused funds from the prior year may roll over.
Any unused amount above $660 will be forfeited.
Transit FSA
A Transit FSA allows use of pre-tax dollars to pay for transit expenses like train, bus, or ferry passes which lowers taxable income.
Parking FSA
A Parking FSA allows you to pay for eligible work-related parking expenses at your place of employment with pre-tax dollars, such as ramp or park-and-ride.

rows.label: Under 55

You may use pre-tax dollars from your Dependent Care FSA to pay for expenses for care of a dependent child under 13, or child not capable of self care, or elderly parent inside your home, and expenses outside home such as babysitters, nursery schools, or daycare centers.

benefitChapters.title: Survivor Benefits
benefitChapters.description: If you are a full-time or part-time benefits eligible employee, RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage through Mutual of Omaha.

children.text: If you are a full-time or part-time benefits eligible employee, RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage through Mutual of Omaha.
RS&H provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment Insurance (AD&D)
If you are a full-time or part-time benefits eligible employee, RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage through Mutual of Omaha.
Coverage amount
Basic Life and AD&D coverage is equal to your annual salary up to $1,000,000 and is provided at no cost to you.
At age 65, your life insurance amount begins to reduce by a percentage of the original coverage amount: 35% at 65, 45% at 70, 70% at 75, and 80% at 80.
AD&D Coverage
AD&D coverage provides benefits in case of death by accident or dismemberment, paying in addition to life insurance in the event of accidental death.
Death: AD&D benefit pays in addition to life insurance if death is caused by accident.
Dismemberment: pays a percentage of total AD&D benefit if losing a covered body part or function due to accident.
Other Voluntary benefits
Voluntary Life and AD&D insurance are available for additional coverage for you, your spouse, and your dependents. Evidence of Insurability required if enrolling beyond guaranteed issue amounts.
Voluntary Associate Life: increments of $10,000 up to $450,000 (guaranteed issue $250,000).
Voluntary Spouse Life: increments of $5,000 up to $225,000 (guaranteed issue $50,000).
Voluntary Child Life: increments of $5,000 up to $10,000 (no guaranteed issue).
Voluntary AD&D insurance: available with maximum coverage $500,000, family coverage at percentages of your coverage (spouse 50%, child 15%, spouse & child 40%/10%).
Designating Beneficiaries
You must designate a beneficiary for your life insurance coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Supplemental Health
benefitChapters.description: Supplemental health benefits provided by Mutual of Omaha.

children.text: Provider: Mutual of Omaha
What is Supplemental Health?
Supplemental health plans work alongside your medical insurance to help fill financial gaps when unexpected events happen.
These benefits are offered through Mutual of Omaha.
Critical Illness Coverage
Critical Illness coverage pays a lump sum benefit regardless of other medical insurance, and covers illnesses like Cancer, Heart Attack, Stroke, and more.
No medical questions required to elect coverage.
Coverage can be taken with you if you leave or retire (to age 70).
Family coverage is available; children covered at no additional cost.
$50 Health Screening Benefit per person per year.
Covered illness include:
Cancer
Stroke
Advanced Parkinson’s
Heart Attack
Major Organ Transplant
Advanced Alzheimer’s
Coronary Bypass Surgery
End Stage Renal Failure
Childhood and Developmental conditions
Payment frequency
Different diagnoses: pays per illness with at least 6 months separation.
Same diagnosis: pays full amount twice with 6 months between diagnoses.
Accident Coverage
Accident insurance pays you based on injuries and treatments from covered accidents, anytime of day, working or not.
Family coverage available, no medical questions required, coverage pays per covered accident with no limit on number of accidents.
You can take this policy with you if you retire or leave RS&H.

rows.label: Health Screening Benefit (Per Person, Per Year)

benefitChapters.title: Income Protection (Disability)
benefitChapters.description: Short-term and long-term disability insurance and income protection benefits.

children.text: RS&H offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance through Mutual of Omaha to provide income protection.
Understand your disability insurance options.
Overview
RS&H offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance through Mutual of Omaha to provide income protection.
Short-Term Disability Insurance Core Plan
Core Plan is provided at no cost to you.
Enrollment is automatic.
A Buy-Up Plan is available for purchase for extra financial protection.
STD Notes

Pre-existing exclusion: You will not be covered for any disability that happens in the first six months of coverage if you received treatment for that condition in the three months before coverage began.
Long term disability insurance
Coverage is provided at no cost to you.

After 7 calendar days of inability to work

An additional 30% of your income to a maximum of $2,000 per week

rows.label: How much it pays

benefitChapters.title: Financial Wellbeing (401k)
benefitChapters.description: The RS&H 401(k) plan allows you to contribute part of your salary toward retirement while deferring taxes on that income. Contributions reduce taxable income. A

children.text: The RS&H 401(k) plan allows you to contribute part of your salary toward retirement while deferring taxes on that income. Contributions reduce taxable income. A Roth 401(k) option lets you contribute post-tax income. All Associates over 18, including temporary and part-time, are eligible.
Eligibility
All Associates over the age of 18, including temporary and part-time, are eligible to participate in the 401(k) plan.
Employee enrollment and contributions

All new associates will be automatically enrolled in the Plan at 6% of your pay (pre-tax) as soon as administratively possible. You have approximately 45 days from your date of hire to contact Fidelity to opt out or change your deferral amount.
Automatically enrolled at 6%.
Contribution in Traditional 401(k) or Roth.
You must make a contribution election to participate in Catch-up Contribution.
If earned more than $145,000 in prior year catch-up contribution must be made into Roth account automatically.
Matching Contribution
RS&H makes a 50% matching contribution on your contributions, up to 6% of your pay.
The match is split: 2/3 is cash contributed each pay period; 1/3 is company stock contributed annually.
An associate earning $75,000 who contributes 6% ($4,500) will have a company match of $2,250; $1,500 in cash and $750 in stock.
Vesting schedule
Your contributions and the growth are always 100% vested.
The company matching contributions vest over a five-year period based on years of service.
Pre-tax 401(k)
Contributions are pre-tax, reducing your taxable income until withdrawal.
Roth 401(k)
Contributions are post-tax, withdrawals are tax-free.
Beneficiaries
Manage your 401(k) beneficiaries at netbenefits.com.
Loans
Loans can be requested if you have a vested balance of $1,000 or more.
Employee Ownership
RS&H promotes employee ownership including stock match, annual offerings, and incentive programs.
Opportunities for Investment
Investment opportunities are available through the Fidelity 401(k) plan.

rows.label: Standard Contribution
cells: Automatically enrolled at 6%. Contribution in Traditional 401(k) or Roth.

rows.label: Catchup Contribution
cells: You must make a contribution election to participate in Catchup Contribution. If earned more than $145,000 in prior year catchup contribution must be made into Roth account (automatic).

rows.label: Super Catchup Contribution
cells: After attaining maximum annual standard and catchup contribution, may be eligible to save additional in an after-tax Roth contribution, which will be automatically converted to a Roth IRA.

rows.label: Mega Roth
cells: May save additional in an after-tax Roth contribution after other limits.

benefitChapters.title: Paid Time Off and Other Benefits
benefitChapters.description: RS&H observes 8 holidays including New Year's Day, Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Day after Thanksgiving,

children.text: RS&H observes 8 holidays including New Year's Day, Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Day after Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day.
Holidays
RS&H observes 8 holidays including New Year's Day, Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Day after Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day.
New Year’s Day
Martin Luther King Jr. Day
Memorial Day
Independence Day
Labor Day
Thanksgiving
Day after Thanksgiving
Christmas Day
Floating Holiday
Benefits eligible associates receive one additional floating holiday per year which can be used any time with prior approval.
Religious Holidays
Associates may use vacation or floating holidays to observe religious holidays not covered among the standard ones, with prior supervisor notice.
Time Away
Vacation allowance is based on tenure and credited on date of hire and January each year.
Accrual Logic
Full vacation allowance is credited at the beginning of each calendar year based on expected full year work.
New hires receive prorated vacation based on hire date.
Part-time associates receive prorated vacation based on hours worked.
Personal Leave (PL)
Personal Leave (PL) is available for personal illness, family emergencies, medical appointments, and other situations. PL days are credited at the start of the year or upon hire.
Leave Bank (LB)
Unused PL is transferred to a Leave Bank with a maximum of 240 hours. Leave Bank is accessed for unpaid FMLA leaves.
Paid Parental Leave (PPL)
Paid Parental Leave (PPL) offers up to 3 weeks of paid leave to care for and bond with a newborn or adopted child.
Up to 2 weeks paid leave per birth or adoption (including surrogacy), regardless of multiple births or adoptions.
Up to 1 additional week paid leave using accrued Leave Bank hours.
Paid Parental Leave is subject to approval.
Bereavement Leave

RS&H provides up to 3 days of paid Bereavement Leave for death or imminent death of immediate family members including spouse, domestic partner, child, parent, step-parent, sibling, grandparents, grandchildren, and similar relatives of spouse/domestic partner.
Jury Duty
Paid leave is provided for jury duty based on standard working hours.
Other PTO Details
Flexible Work Arrangements are available.

rows.label: Less than 1 year
cells: 3.07 hours per payroll period (new hire accrual)
